evals.prompt: I'm in the 24% federal tax bracket, single filer, earning $95,000. I have a 401k at work with a 4% employer match, and I have $10,000 to invest this year. How should I allocate it across account types for maximum tax efficiency?
evals.expected_output: Priority: (1) 401k to capture full 4% match ($3,800 at $95k salary), (2) max Roth IRA ($7,000), (3) remaining into traditional 401k. At 24% bracket, Roth is preferred if future rates expected to be similar or higher. Tax savings from pre-tax contributions highlighted.
assertions: First priority is capturing the full employer match
Recommends maxing Roth IRA at the $7,000 limit
Explains Roth vs. traditional trade-off at 24% bracket
Provides a specific dollar allocation plan

evals.prompt: A municipal bond yields 3.5% tax-free. I'm in the 32% tax bracket. What taxable bond yield would I need to match it?
evals.expected_output: Tax-equivalent yield = 3.5% / (1 − 0.32) = 5.15%. A taxable bond must yield at least 5.15% to match the after-tax value of the 3.5% muni bond for this investor.

evals.prompt: From the attached tax inputs (evals/files/tax_inputs.csv), calculate the after-tax future value of the investment in a taxable account vs. a Roth IRA over the specified horizon. Quantify the tax drag in dollar terms.
evals.expected_output: Roth IRA FV ~$54,274; Taxable FV ~$42,415; tax drag cost ~$11,859 over 25 years; Roth advantage ~21.8% more wealth.
assertions: Tax drag cost stated in dollars
